Defines the built-in properties and methods for the Array.prototype object in ECMAScript.

# Purpose
The code defines the built-in properties and methods for the `Array.prototype` object in a JavaScript engine. It specifies the object identifier for `Array.prototype` using the macro `OBJECT_ID` and defines its properties and methods using macros such as `OBJECT_VALUE`, `NUMBER_VALUE`, and `ROUTINE`. These macros associate property names with their corresponding C function implementations and define attributes like configurability and writability.

The code includes a series of `ROUTINE` definitions that map JavaScript array methods to their C function counterparts. These methods include `toString`, `concat`, `join`, `pop`, `push`, `reverse`, `shift`, `slice`, `sort`, `splice`, `unshift`, `indexOf`, `lastIndexOf`, `every`, `some`, `forEach`, `map`, `filter`, `reduce`, and `reduceRight`. Each routine specifies the number of arguments it accepts and the length property value. This file is part of a larger system that implements JavaScript functionality in C, providing a bridge between JavaScript method calls and their execution in a C environment.
